label: handle-demand-spawns-on-any-request
detail: handleModelServerDemand computes routePath but uses it only in logger() lines; no allowlist, no method/path guard. After the give-up-cooldown check, every request falls through to getSupervisor().launch(). So GET /api/health to a cold demand listener spawns the server. T1 CONFIRMED.

label: probe-payload-is-literal-get-api-health
detail: probeModelServer builds 'GET /api/health HTTP/1.1...' from MODEL_SERVER_HEALTH_PATH='/api/health' and writes it on socket connect. This exact request lands on whatever is listening at the socket.

label: trigger-requires-launcher-overlap
detail: prepareModelServerDemandTarget at boot probes a pre-existing socket; if it returns alive it backs off. The spawn case is the boot probe GET landing on a live SIBLING launcher running only a COLD demand listener (not a real server), whose handleModelServerDemand then spawns. Needs two overlapping launchers; a single sequential boot finds no live sibling listener and does not trip it.

label: DECISIVE-real-embed-path-wakes-via-get-api-health
detail: embedPrepared (real embed path) calls waitForReady() before the embed whenever the ready-latch is stale/missing; waitForReady loops healthOnce() which issues GET /api/health; the embed itself is POST /api/embed issued only after readiness. So /api/health IS the genuine consumer wake/readiness step. Making /api/health non-spawning would break real hf-local hosts: a consumer readiness GET to a cold listener would 503 forever and POST /api/embed would never get a live server.

label: adr-014-treats-health-as-liveness-selection-probe
detail: ADR-014 bootstrap chooses hf-local when its Node model server ANSWERS /api/health (ready or loading both count) — the probe is a READ of liveness for cascade selection, expecting an already-alive/booting launcher-supervised server. Sidecars are lazy/cold by design (forked on first embedding request, idle-evicted). Design intends on-demand spawn tied to a real EMBED demand, not to a bare liveness read. Current code conflates read-liveness with demand-spawn — the precise T1 design violation.

label: 010-packet-dir-absent-docs-authoritative
detail: 010-embedding-consolidation-hf-local-server is not physically present under the 003-memory-and-causal-runtime arc (renumbered/consolidated). The canonical ADR-014 references (embedding_resilience.md, embedder_architecture.md) are the authoritative design-intent substitute and were used instead.

label: probe-must-not-equal-embed-demand
detail: A bare launcher liveness probe must NOT spawn the model server, but a genuine consumer's wake (currently GET /api/health then POST /api/embed) must still spawn a cold server. The probe-vs-demand distinction cannot be drawn on the /api/health path/method alone (F3-4). Leaning: launcher probeModelServer carries a dedicated probe marker header that handleModelServerDemand treats as read-only/non-spawning.

label: boot-probe-also-launcher-internal
detail: prepareModelServerDemandTarget's own boot probe is launcher-internal, so a probe-marker header on probeModelServer would cover both the boot liveness probe and any future internal probe; it should be exempted from spawning the same way as a consumer's bare liveness read.

label: idle-monitor-not-a-spawn-source
detail: tickIdleMonitor early-returns unless getPid() records a live resident and never calls launch(); it only reaps/re-arms the listener. Spawn-safe, ruled out as a spurious-spawn source.

label: suppress-health-is-not-the-fix
detail: Making /api/health non-spawning is NOT a valid T1 fix: genuine consumers wake via that exact GET (F3-4); suppressing it breaks real hf-local hosts. The fix must discriminate probe-vs-demand another way (marker header).
